findings.evidence: grep hits: `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Stream/Defs.lean:20:def Stream' (α : Type u) := ℕ → α`, `:28:def cons`, `:40:def drop`, `:42:def tail`; `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Stream/Init.lean:49:theorem tail_cons`.
findings.fix: Replace these definitions with direct use of `Stream'.drop`, `Stream'.cons`, and `Stream'.tail` (or make any local notation an abbrev/thin compatibility layer stated in terms of them), and reuse `Stream'.tail_cons` for the cons-tail normal form.
findings.issue: `processShift`, `processCons`, and `processTail` are parallel wrappers for Mathlib's `Stream'` API on `ℕ → α`, creating duplicate names for stream drop/cons/tail.

findings.evidence: grep hit: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Pi.lean:81:lemma prod_indicator_apply ... ∏ i ∈ s, (f i).indicator ... = (⋂ x ∈ s, f x).indicator ...`, with `prod_indicator` at line 92.
findings.fix: Rewrite each factor with the indicator-preimage/comp lemma and prove this from `Finset.prod_indicator` or `Finset.prod_indicator_apply`, then identify the resulting finite intersection with `prefixCylinder`.
findings.issue: `indProd_eq_indicator` reproves the generic finite product-of-indicators identity for the special sets `{ω | X i ω ∈ C i}`.

summary: The PR adds useful support shapes, but part of the API duplicates existing Mathlib stream and indicator-product infrastructure. Reuse those declarations instead of introducing parallel sequence combinators and reproving the generic product-of-indicators lemma.
